title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — June 21, 2026
headline: Humana's nH Predict AI denial system and a DOJ False Claims Act complaint alleging disability discrimination in Medicare Advantage enrollment drive a band-crossing proposal from Functional to Developing (-5.4), the June 2026 series' most methodologically consequential pending finding — while Sudan's RSF massing around El Obeid prompts a formal UN Security Council demand to halt and the UN rights chief's public call to 'stop this madness,' and Afghanistan's Taliban open fire on Herat women's-rights protesters, killing a child.
summary: June 21 is a single-proposal cycle across 20 assessments in 3 indexes, producing 1 band-crossing proposal (PENDING) and 19 confirmations with 7 floor/near-floor reinforcements. The lead finding is Humana (fortune-500): a proposed downgrade from 40.6 Functional to 35.2 Developing (-5.4), driven by two independent federal/judicial evidence channels — the DOJ False Claims Act complaint alleging Humana conspired to discriminate against disabled Medicare beneficiaries (Tier 5, May 2025) and the Barrows v. Humana AI-denial class action surviving a motion to dismiss (June 2026). The proposal is documented at the Developing-band level but held pending because pre-adjudication discipline cautions against crossing a band on unadjudicated evidence; the largest driver is a 13-month-old pleaded complaint, and the class-action dismissal-survival is procedural, not a merits finding. This mirrors the June 18 within-band downgrade discipline and the boundary watch that followed. The conversion triggers are precise: a DOJ FCA ruling or settlement, or a Barrows merits ruling, would resolve this cycle's open finding. The floor cluster accumulates its most severe simultaneous reinforcement of the June series: Afghanistan at 0.0 following Taliban lethal force on Herat women's-rights protesters (a child killed, 30+ women arrested); Sudan at 0.0 with the UN Security Council issuing a formal demand to halt the RSF assault on El Obeid and the UN rights chief issuing a direct public call to stop; xAI/Grok at 0.0 with the OPC Canada adjudicated privacy finding upgrading its evidence from allegation to confirmed finding; DRC at 2.3 with Ebola now at 896 cases and 232 deaths heading toward its July natural peak, with M23 forced recruitment overlapping geographically; Bolivia at 6.3 entering a declared state of emergency with 17 deaths including 7 from blocked medical access. Anthropic confirms at 59.1 on a methodologically clean application of the victim/perpetrator rule: the US Commerce Department's export-control order suspending Fable 5/Mythos 5 for foreign nationals is government action inflicted on Anthropic, not an Anthropic conduct failure. DeepMind/Google confirms at 56.9 on its AI Control Roadmap publication. Apple enters its third consecutive cycle on the Towson NLRB I3 watch flag.

topSignals.title: Humana Band-Crossing Proposed: 40.6 Functional → 35.2 Developing on DOJ Disability-Discrimination Complaint and AI Denial Class Action
topSignals.description: A proposed Functional-to-Developing band crossing (-5.4) for Humana rests on two independent federal/judicial evidence channels. First, the DOJ False Claims Act complaint (Tier 5, filed May 2025) alleges Humana conspired with Medicare Advantage brokers to discriminate against beneficiaries with disabilities — threatening to withhold kickbacks to pressure brokers to enroll fewer disabled members — a direct government-pleaded allegation of deliberate equity harm to Humana's most vulnerable population. Second, Barrows v. Humana survived a motion to dismiss (June 15, 2026), alleging that Humana's nH Predict AI algorithm knowingly overrides treating physicians' determinations of medically necessary post-acute care for elderly members. The four dimensions most affected: EQU drops from 2.0 to 1.5 (the largest single dimensional move, reflecting the government-pleaded disability discrimination); ACC drops from 2.5 to 2.0 (Humana is contesting both actions with no acknowledgment or reparative action); EMP, ACT, and INT each decline 0.25 raw points. AWR, BND, and SYS remain unchanged. Sector corroboration: CVS/Aetna paid a $117.7M Medicare Advantage settlement this cycle, consistent with the documented MA denial-and-discrimination pattern. The proposal is documented this cycle as a Developing-band finding. The conversion triggers are specific: a DOJ FCA adjudication or settlement, or a Barrows class-action merits ruling, would resolve the pending finding. This is the third Humana-related action in the June 2026 series: boundary-watch established at 40.6 on June 18 following the applied -11.0 downgrade; second consecutive cycle with the Functional/Developing boundary under active evidence accumulation.

evidence.quote: The complaint alleges that Aetna and Humana each conspired with the brokers to discriminate against Medicare beneficiaries with disabilities whom they perceived to be less profitable. Aetna and Humana allegedly threatened to withhold kickbacks to pressure brokers to enroll fewer disabled Medicare beneficiaries in their plans.
evidence.claim: DOJ alleges Humana deliberately sought to enroll fewer disabled Medicare beneficiaries — a government-pleaded direct equity harm to its most vulnerable members.
evidence.source: U.S. Department of Justice, Office of Public Affairs

evidence.quote: Plaintiffs Joanne Barrows and Susan Hagood claim Humana uses AI in place of human doctors to override live physicians determinations as to medically necessary care patients require.
evidence.claim: Humana's nH Predict AI model is alleged to override treating physicians to deny medically necessary post-acute care to elderly members.
evidence.source: Georgetown Health Care Litigation Tracker (Barrows et al. v. Humana, Inc.)

evidence.quote: The United States alleges that from 2016 through at least 2021, the defendant insurers paid hundreds of millions of dollars in illegal kickbacks to the defendant brokers in exchange for enrollments into the insurers' Medicare Advantage plans.
evidence.claim: Humana is among the insurers named in a DOJ complaint alleging hundreds of millions in illegal Medicare Advantage kickbacks.
evidence.source: U.S. Department of Justice, Office of Public Affairs

topSignals.title: Sudan 0.0 Reinforced: UN Security Council Demands RSF Halt El Obeid Assault — 'Stop This Madness,' Says UN Rights Chief
topSignals.description: The UN Security Council issued a formal demand on June 20 requiring the RSF to halt its assault on El Obeid, North Kordofan, where approximately 500,000 civilians face an imminent large-scale atrocity. The UN High Commissioner for Human Rights issued a direct public statement calling on the RSF to 'stop this madness.' The UNSC demand represents an escalation above the 29-nation UNHRC warning documented on June 19 — moving from multilateral concern to formal Security Council action. RSF forces are documented to be massing around the city. Sudan holds at its absolute harm-flag floor of 0.0 with no further composite movement available; the monitoring significance is the precision of the risk: a named city, a documented assault pattern, a formal UNSC demand, and a specific civilian population count. The Sudan situation now carries the most immediate and precisely specified mass-atrocity risk of any entity in the current monitoring portfolio.

topSignals.title: Afghanistan 0.0 Reinforced: Taliban Open Fire on Herat Women's-Rights Protesters, Kill a Child — 30+ Women Arrested
topSignals.description: Taliban security forces opened fire into a crowd of women's-rights protesters in Herat on June 9, killing a child. Thirty or more women were subsequently arrested. The documented use of lethal force against peaceful women's-rights demonstrators — killing a minor — is the most severe single event in the Afghanistan floor cluster in the current monitoring window. Afghanistan confirms at its absolute harm-flag floor of 0.0; the event reinforces the evidence tier behind the floor designation without generating further composite delta. The Herat shooting is categorically distinct from the protest-policing events documented in prior cycles: it involves the killing of a child in a crowd of women protesters, a combination that crosses multiple independent harm-floor anchors simultaneously (lethal force against children, lethal force against women's-rights protesters, gender-persecutory state conduct).

topSignals.title: Anthropic 59.1 Confirmed: Commerce Department Export-Control Order Is Government Action on Anthropic — Victim/Perpetrator Rule Applies
topSignals.description: The US Commerce Department's export-control order forced Anthropic to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for foreign nationals. This is government-imposed action inflicted on Anthropic — the perpetrator is the federal government, not Anthropic. Under the victim/perpetrator attribution convention, conduct inflicted on an entity by a documented external actor is attributed to that actor and not scored against the entity subject. Anthropic complied transparently with the order while contesting its scope through appropriate legal channels — neutral-to-mildly-positive own conduct. The assessment is high confidence: the factual classification is unambiguous. Anthropic confirms at 59.1 Functional for the third consecutive cycle. This is the first application of the victim/perpetrator convention to a constraint imposed on an AI lab by the US government in the June 2026 series; prior applications covered Ukraine (Russian strikes, 6 cycles) and the University Index (federal funding actions, 10 entities, June 20).

topSignals.title: DRC 2.3 Confirmed: Ebola Reaches 896 Cases / 232 Deaths — M23 Forced Recruitment Overlaps Outbreak Geography
topSignals.description: The DRC Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak has reached 896 confirmed cases and 232 deaths as of June 21 data, advancing toward its July natural peak window. Contact tracing is documented at approximately 45% of estimated exposures — an improvement from earlier cycles but still well below the 80% threshold needed for outbreak containment. The cross-border spread risk toward Uganda is now documented and tracked by WHO. A distinct but geographically overlapping crisis: HRW has documented M23/RDF forced recruitment, use of child soldiers, and extrajudicial executions in the DRC's eastern provinces — these harms are attributed to Rwanda as the external state actor (per the victim/perpetrator convention, with M23 functioning as Rwanda's proxy force), not scored against DRC. DRC's own conduct confirms at the near-floor state-capacity baseline of 2.3 Critical. The July natural Ebola peak window is approximately 5 weeks away; no approved Bundibugyo-strain vaccine exists.

topSignals.title: xAI/Grok 0.0 Reinforced: OPC Canada Adjudicated Finding Upgrades Evidence from Allegation to Confirmed Privacy Violation
topSignals.description: The Office of the Privacy Commissioner of Canada issued an adjudicated finding that xAI violated Canadian privacy law through non-consensual deepfake generation, including 23,000 or more child images. This upgrades the xAI/Grok evidentiary record from allegation-grade to adjudicated-finding-grade — the most significant evidence-tier change for the floor entity since the Devin Kim whistleblower suit documented in June 19. The OPC ruling is a Tier-5 government adjudication; it confirms the conduct pattern with the authority of a national regulatory body rather than a complainant. xAI/Grok confirms at the absolute 0.0 harm-flag floor; the floor remains secure on three independent evidence streams (whistleblower suit alleging internal safety suppression, UK MP deepfake litigation, and now the OPC adjudicated finding). The OPC finding is also independently significant for the AI labs index: it is the first national-regulator adjudicated privacy finding against any AI lab entity in the current monitoring record.

evidence.claim: The OPC Canada adjudicated finding documents that xAI's privacy violations — including non-consensual deepfake generation covering 23,000+ child images — were confirmed by a national regulatory body and found to be ongoing.
evidence.source: Office of the Privacy Commissioner of Canada (OPC)

topSignals.title: DeepMind/Google 56.9 Confirmed: AI Control Roadmap Published — Rogue-Agent Defenses and Live Gemini Spark Monitor Documented
topSignals.description: DeepMind/Google published its AI Control Roadmap on June 18, documenting rogue-agent defenses and a live monitoring system for the Gemini Spark deployment. The roadmap constitutes a genuine anticipatory-awareness and systemic-thinking artifact: it addresses failure modes in deployed AI systems through a documented institutional commitment, not a one-time report. The AWR and SYS dimensions benefit most directly — the Roadmap is the type of proactive harm-pathway documentation that distinguishes the upper tier of the AI labs index. DeepMind/Google confirms at 56.9 Functional with high confidence. In context of the June 2026 AI labs assessments, DeepMind and Anthropic (59.1) are now the two most thoroughly documented labs at the dimension level; both confirm this cycle on the strength of institutional safety infrastructure. The contrast with xAI/Grok (0.0, OPC adjudicated violation) and Meta AI (26.3, KOSA lobbying sub-threshold) is the sharpest bifurcation in the AI labs index in the current series.

topSignals.title: Bolivia 6.3 Confirmed: State of Emergency Declared June 20 — 17 Deaths Including 7 from Blocked Medical Access
topSignals.description: Bolivia's President Arce declared a state of emergency on June 20 as the protest-blockade crisis reached 17 confirmed deaths, including 7 attributable to blocked medical access — patients unable to reach hospitals, dialysis centers, or emergency care. The declared state of emergency escalates the government response posture but does not change the harm attribution: the 7 blockade-caused medical-access deaths are primarily attributable to the protest actors blocking roads. The state's own conduct remains the subject of assessment — emergency powers declared but the army has not yet been deployed against protesters. Bolivia confirms at 6.3 Critical near-floor. The conversion trigger for a floor-designation proposal remains unchanged: military-on-civilian casualties under the June 15 state-of-exception law.

boundaryWatch.trigger: DOJ False Claims Act complaint (May 2025, Tier 5) alleging disability discrimination in Medicare Advantage enrollment; Barrows v. Humana AI-denial class action surviving dismissal (June 15, 2026, Tier 4); composite proposed at 35.2 Developing. Largest driver: EQU 2.0 → 1.5 on government-pleaded discrimination against disabled beneficiaries.
boundaryWatch.note: Humana sits 0.6 points above the Functional/Developing boundary at its published composite of 40.6. A band-crossing proposal to 35.2 Developing is documented this cycle on two independent federal/judicial channels. The DOJ FCA complaint is 13 months old and unadjudicated; Barrows survived a dismissal motion but has not yet reached a merits ruling. The conversion pathway runs through DOJ FCA adjudication or settlement, or a Barrows class-action merits decision.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Bundibugyo Ebola 896 cases / 232 deaths; July natural peak window approximately 5 weeks away; contact tracing ~45%; no approved Bundibugyo vaccine; M23 forced recruitment overlapping outbreak geography (M23/RDF harm attributed to Rwanda as perpetrator).
boundaryWatch.note: DRC at 2.3 Critical through cycle 8 of the Ebola outbreak. Contact tracing improvement from below 20% to approximately 45% is a positive trend; it remains below the containment threshold. The geographic overlap of M23-controlled territory with Ebola response zones is an emerging operational risk for the WHO-led response — if M23 or state forces impede WHO burial teams or response corridors, it would constitute state-adjacent obstruction, the conversion trigger for a floor-designation proposal from 2.3.

boundaryWatch.trigger: State of emergency declared June 20; 17 dead (7 from blocked medical access); army authorized but not deployed against protesters under June 15 state-of-exception law.
boundaryWatch.note: Bolivia at 6.3 Critical as a declared state of emergency enters force. The harm pathway through week 9 of the crisis remains obstruction-of-care, consistent with the ACT and SYS dimension scoring. The state-of-exception law is now active; military-on-civilian casualties under this legal authority constitute the conversion trigger for a floor-designation proposal. The army's non-deployment against protesters is a restraint signal that has held through the escalation to state-of-emergency status.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Bengali Muslim pushbacks continuing; detect-delete-deport digital surveillance of Rohingya and Bengali Muslim communities; 200+ pushback events documented since June 1; seventh consecutive cycle on active watch at 0.6pt above the Critical floor.
boundaryWatch.note: India at 15.6 Critical sits 0.6 points above the Critical floor. The Bengali Muslim pushback pattern — now seven consecutive cycles of documented evidence at this boundary distance — is accumulating across overlapping enforcement mechanisms (physical deportation, digital identification, detention). The floor conversion trigger is a large-scale documented atrocity or a formal adjudicated finding of statelessness-through-deportation at scale.

emergingRisks.risk: Sudan's El Obeid situation has now reached formal UN Security Council action — the most precisely specified imminent mass-atrocity risk in the current monitoring portfolio, with approximately 500,000 civilians at documented risk of RSF assault.
emergingRisks.detail: The UNSC demand and the UN rights chief's public statement on June 20 elevate the multilateral warning to its highest institutional tier. Sudan holds at 0.0 with no further composite movement. The monitoring significance is the precision of the risk specification: a named city, a documented assault-preparation pattern, formal Security Council action, and a specific civilian population count. Compounding effects on Nigeria's famine response capacity and international humanitarian attention to DRC's Ebola outbreak are the secondary monitoring concerns.

emergingRisks.risk: DRC Bundibugyo Ebola is accelerating toward the July natural peak window with contact tracing still below containment thresholds, no approved vaccine, and M23-controlled territory beginning to overlap with outbreak response corridors.
emergingRisks.detail: 896 cases / 232 deaths as of June 21; contact tracing at approximately 45% (improved from earlier <20% but below the 80% containment threshold); no approved Bundibugyo vaccine. The geographic overlap of M23/RDF controlled areas with Ebola response zones in North Kivu and Ituri is an emerging operational risk: M23-imposed movement restrictions on burial teams or response personnel would constitute a secondary obstruction pathway separate from DRC state conduct. Rwanda's documentation in M23 command-and-control (HRW, June 21) means this risk is attributed at the Rwanda level. July peak is the primary forward checkpoint.

emergingRisks.risk: Humana's nH Predict AI denial system continues to accumulate adverse federal and judicial evidence; the DOJ FCA and Barrows proceedings now constitute the June 2026 series' most developed pre-adjudication evidence base for a non-floor entity.
emergingRisks.detail: The proposed 40.6 → 35.2 Developing band crossing rests on a DOJ FCA complaint now 13 months old and a class-action dismissal-survival from June 15. FCA cases typically resolve over multi-year timescales; the Barrows merits phase will generate discovery evidence relevant to future assessments. The CVS/Aetna $117.7M sector settlement documents an adjacent conduct category with an adjudicated resolution — sector pressure for Humana FCA resolution may accelerate. A DOJ settlement in the range of the CVS/Aetna amount would constitute a Tier-5 adjudicated finding and resolve the pending band-crossing proposal.

emergingRisks.risk: EU AI Act full applicability on August 2 arrives 42 days out, with documented safety-governance gaps at three AI labs index entities and no public compliance roadmap published by any of the three.
emergingRisks.detail: Figure AI (31.3, applied June 19), OpenAI (27.5, confirmed June 19), and xAI/Grok (0.0, floor) each have documented evidence mapping onto Article 9 EU AI Act risk-management system requirements for high-risk and GPAI systems. The August 2 deadline is firm; non-compliance by EU-facing entities constitutes a Tier-5 regulatory finding in the post-applicability period. Contrast: DeepMind/Google (56.9) published its AI Control Roadmap June 18, which maps onto Article 9 at a documented institutional level.

emergingRisks.risk: UnitedHealth Group's DOJ criminal probe now spans three simultaneous revenue streams; any adjudicated finding in Medicare Advantage, Optum Rx, or physician reimbursement would move UHG from the near-floor absorption state to a floor-designation proposal.
emergingRisks.detail: The three-probe scope (Medicare Advantage billing, Optum Rx PBM, physician reimbursement) and the Senate Grassley finding of 'aggressive' risk-score gaming collectively represent the most extensive concurrent documented probe against a managed-care entity in the benchmark's record. UHG at 10.2 near-floor Critical absorbs all pre-adjudication evidence; an indictment or guilty plea in any single branch converts absorption to proposal generation.

emergingRisks.risk: Apple's Towson NLRB I3 watch flag enters its third consecutive assessment cycle; adjudication of the IAM unfair-labor-practice charge as an adverse ruling would establish the first direct-actor I3 finding in the Apple Fortune 500 record.
emergingRisks.detail: Apple's published 59.4 Functional rests substantially on supply-chain responsibility and worker-program infrastructure at the indirect-actor level. The Towson IAM NLRB charge — alleged transfer-rights disparity between unionized and non-union store closures — is categorically a direct-actor I3 event if adjudicated adversely. The I3 watch flag has now been active for three consecutive cycles on the same underlying event sequence (store closure June 20, NLRB charge June 20, continued pre-adjudication watch June 21).

sectorAlerts.sector: Fortune 500 — Healthcare / Managed Care: Pre-Adjudication Band-Crossing Documentation
sectorAlerts.headline: The Humana band-crossing proposal illustrates the managed-care sector's convergence of AI-driven denial systems and federal enforcement — two independent evidence channels meeting the corroboration threshold for proposal generation
observations: The DOJ FCA complaint and the Barrows class-action survival are the first instance in the June 2026 series where two independent federal/judicial channels simultaneously address the same entity's conduct in the same service category (Medicare Advantage denial and enrollment discrimination).

EQU drops from 2.0 to 1.5 — the largest dimensional move this cycle — driven by a government pleading of deliberate discrimination against disabled beneficiaries. This is categorically distinct from operational access barriers: the DOJ alleges intentional profit-driven adverse selection.

The CVS/Aetna $117.7M Medicare Advantage settlement provides sector corroboration: two of the three largest Medicare Advantage insurers now have documented adverse federal proceedings in the current monitoring window, with one ($117.7M settlement) resolved and one (Humana FCA complaint) pending.

UnitedHealth Group (10.2 Critical) and Humana (40.6 Functional, proposed 35.2 Developing) represent the two extreme endpoints of the managed-care sub-sector within the Fortune 500 index — both on active adverse federal tracks in the same June assessment window.

sectorAlerts.sector: AI Labs — Bifurcation Between Safety Leaders and Floor Entities Sharpens
sectorAlerts.headline: DeepMind/Google's AI Control Roadmap and Anthropic's export-control compliance confirm the top tier of the AI labs index as genuinely differentiated; the OPC Canada ruling against xAI deepens the floor-cluster evidence base
observations: DeepMind/Google (56.9) and Anthropic (59.1) both confirm this cycle on the strength of documented safety infrastructure: the AI Control Roadmap (proactive rogue-agent defenses) and the export-control compliance and legal contest (transparent institutional response to government imposition), respectively.

xAI/Grok (0.0) now has three independent evidence streams at floor: the Devin Kim whistleblower suit (alleged internal safety suppression), the UK MP deepfake suit, and the OPC Canada adjudicated privacy finding documenting 23,000+ child images — the first national-regulator adjudicated finding against any AI lab in the current monitoring record.

Meta AI (26.3 published, 26.2 re-score) continues its anti-accountability pattern via KOSA Section 230 lobbying; the delta is sub-threshold but the ACC and INT dimensions are both in evidence-tier upgrade territory.

The gap between the top (Anthropic 59.1) and the floor (xAI/Grok 0.0) is 59.1 points — the widest inter-entity spread within a single index in the June 2026 series, with documented institutional reasons at both extremes.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Floor Cluster: Seven Entities Reinforce Simultaneously
sectorAlerts.headline: Seven floor and near-floor country entities accumulate adverse evidence on June 21, with the Sudan UNSC demand and the Afghanistan lethal-force event as the most severe new developments
observations: Sudan (0.0): UNSC formal demand to halt RSF assault; UN rights chief public statement; RSF massing around El Obeid (~500,000 civilians). This is the highest institutional-tier multilateral response to any single event in the June 2026 series.

Afghanistan (0.0): Taliban lethal force against women's-rights protesters in Herat — a child killed, 30+ women arrested. This event is the most severe documented Taliban conduct since the benchmark began monitoring Afghanistan.

xAI/Grok (0.0): OPC Canada adjudicated privacy finding upgrades the AI floor entity's evidentiary basis from allegation to confirmed regulatory finding — methodologically significant as the first national-regulator adjudication in the AI labs floor cluster.
DRC (2.3): Ebola 896/232 with M23 geographic overlap; Rwanda attribution applies to M23 harms but creates operational risk for the WHO-led response through territory control.
Bolivia (6.3): State of emergency declared June 20; 17 dead including 7 from blocked medical access; ninth week of crisis.

sectorAlerts.sector: Fortune 500 — Energy/Refining Rotation Backfill: Five Entities Confirm at Developing Baseline
sectorAlerts.headline: HollyFrontier, Delek, PBF Energy, Calumet, and FirstCash Holdings rotate through at low-confidence Developing baseline — consistent with prior energy-sector rotation patterns
observations: All five energy/refining rotation entities confirm at 23.4 Developing — the placeholder-grade composite assigned absent dimensional scoring evidence.

Historical violation records (EPA Clean Air Act, OSHA fatal-incident citations, Martinez fires, CFPB Military Lending Act) are consistent with the Developing baseline but no fresh 14-day window evidence was produced.
HollyFrontier's entity identity (now HF Sinclair) is flagged for reconciliation — the entity name in the index predates the 2022 HF Sinclair rebrand and merger.
Calumet's Montana Renewables pivot is noted as a mild forward positive but insufficient to move the baseline absent dimensional evidence.

methodologyNotes.name: BAND-CROSSING-PRE-ADJUDICATION (TWO-CHANNEL CORROBORATION) — Humana, June 21
methodologyNotes.description: The Humana band-crossing proposal is documented at the Developing-band level this cycle. It rests on two independent federal/judicial evidence channels (DOJ FCA complaint, Tier 5; Barrows class-action dismissal-survival, Tier 4) plus sector corroboration (CVS/Aetna $117.7M settlement). The proposal is held pending because the largest evidence driver (DOJ FCA complaint) is 13 months old and unadjudicated, and the class-action survival is procedural rather than a merits finding. Pre-adjudication discipline is applied consistently: the benchmark held UnitedHealth Group at near-floor even as its probe scope expanded across three revenue streams, and held Apple at 59.4 through three consecutive cycles on the Towson NLRB charge. What distinguishes the Humana proposal is corroboration depth across two independent channels addressing the same service-category conduct — a standard the methodology treats as sufficient for proposal generation but not for definitive apply without adjudication. The conversion triggers are specific: a DOJ FCA ruling or settlement, or a Barrows class-action merits ruling. This is the methodology's first application of a two-channel pre-adjudication band-crossing proposal in the June 2026 series.

methodologyNotes.name: VICTIM/PERPETRATOR CONVENTION — APPLIED TO AI LAB REGULATORY ACTION (Anthropic, June 21)
methodologyNotes.description: The US Commerce Department's export-control order suspending Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models for foreign nationals is external regulatory action inflicted on Anthropic by the federal government. Under the victim/perpetrator attribution convention, this is attributed to the government as perpetrator, not scored against Anthropic. Anthropic's own conduct response — compliance plus legal contest — is neutral-to-mildly-positive. This is the June 2026 series' first application of the convention to a US government regulatory constraint on an AI lab. Prior applications: Ukraine (Russian strikes, 6 cycles), University Index (federal funding actions, 10 entities, June 20). The principle is consistent: an entity's assessment reflects its own conduct, not harms imposed on it by external actors.

methodologyNotes.name: FLOOR-LIMITATION — SEVEN ENTITIES CONFIRMED (Afghanistan, Sudan, xAI/Grok, DRC, Bolivia, Nigeria, Iran)
methodologyNotes.description: Seven entities confirm at floor or near-floor this cycle under the floor-limitation methodology. Fresh adverse evidence reinforces the priced conduct pattern but cannot generate further composite delta when there is no dimensional headroom below current values. The June 21 floor cluster includes two absolute floor events of particular severity: the Taliban shooting of a child in Herat (Afghanistan 0.0) and the UNSC demand against RSF assault on El Obeid (Sudan 0.0). Both are evidence-tier upgrades reinforcing existing floor designations. xAI/Grok (0.0) receives the June 2026 series' first national-regulator adjudicated finding (OPC Canada), upgrading its evidence tier from allegation-grade to confirmed-finding-grade. The floor-limitation methodology applies to all seven: the evidence reinforces the designation; the score cannot decrease further.

methodologyNotes.name: PRE-ADJUDICATION EVIDENCE-TIER UPGRADE — M23/RDF RWANDA ATTRIBUTION (DRC, June 21)
methodologyNotes.description: HRW's June 21 documentation of M23/RDF forced recruitment, child soldier use, and extrajudicial executions in DRC's eastern provinces is attributed to Rwanda as the external state actor exercising command-and-control over M23 forces. This is consistent with the June 21 Rwanda assessment (27.3 confirmed; re-score 25.0, delta -2.3 sub-threshold) and with the established ACTIVE-COMPLICITY-IN-MASS-ATROCITY-BY-PROXY methodology applied to Rwanda in prior cycles. DRC's own near-floor state-capacity at 2.3 is assessed separately and confirmed at its published composite. The M23 geographic overlap with the Ebola response zone is documented as an emerging operational risk for the WHO-led response, not a DRC own-conduct failure (absent documented DRC obstruction of response).

forwardTriggers.trigger: UNSC formal demand to halt RSF assault on El Obeid (June 20); RSF massing around ~500,000 civilians; UN rights chief public statement. Sudan at absolute 0.0 floor; monitoring significance is the most precisely defined near-term mass-atrocity risk in the current portfolio and its compounding effects on the regional humanitarian system.

July natural Bundibugyo Ebola peak window; 896 cases / 232 deaths as of June 21; contact tracing approximately 45% (below 80% containment threshold); no approved Bundibugyo vaccine; M23 territory overlapping outbreak response corridors. State obstruction of WHO-led response is the conversion trigger for a floor-designation proposal from 2.3.

DOJ False Claims Act ruling or settlement (Aetna/Elevance/Humana/broker complaint, filed May 2025); OR Barrows v. Humana merits ruling on nH Predict AI denial class action. Either event constitutes the conversion trigger for the proposed 40.6 → 35.2 Developing band crossing. The proposal is documented this cycle pending one of these resolutions.

August 2026 lean-season IPC update — primary checkpoint for Borno State Phase 5 famine trajectory and state response adequacy. Secondary: resolution of ISWAP insurgency pressure on humanitarian-access corridors.

DOJ indictment or settlement admitting misconduct in Medicare Advantage billing, Optum Rx, or physician reimbursement. Any single adjudicated outcome moves UHG from the near-floor absorption state to a floor-designation proposal. Senate Grassley investigation findings already incorporated as evidence-tier upgrade.

forwardTriggers.entity: EU AI Act — AI Labs Index
forwardTriggers.trigger: EU AI Act full applicability for high-risk and GPAI provisions (August 2, 42 days from June 21). Safety-governance gaps documented at Figure AI (31.3, applied), OpenAI (27.5, confirmed), and xAI/Grok (0.0, floor) each map onto Article 9 risk-management system requirements. Post-applicability non-compliance constitutes a Tier-5 regulatory finding.

NLRB adjudication of IAM unfair-labor-practice charge over Towson Town Center closure (transfer-rights disparity between unionized and non-union store closures). Adverse ruling would establish the first direct-actor I3 finding in the Apple Fortune 500 assessment record. Third consecutive cycle on I3 watch.

Military-on-civilian casualties under the June 15 state-of-exception law — conversion trigger for floor-designation proposal from 6.3. State of emergency declared June 20; army authorized but not yet deployed against protesters.

dailyOpeningQuestion.text: When two independent federal channels — a DOJ False Claims Act complaint and a class action surviving dismissal — both document Humana discriminating against disabled Medicare beneficiaries through the nH Predict AI system, does corroboration depth across EQU and ACC dimensions meet the Developing-band threshold absent an adjudicated merits ruling?
dailyOpeningQuestion.tensionFraming: Corroboration depth across two independent channels justifies proposal generation; adjudication caution holds the apply — what evidence resolves the tension?
